title: SSD1306 OLED Display UI Module
design_question: How to show real-time readings (speed, SoC, time, dose count) on a compact low-power display?
selected_approach: 0.96" 128×64 monochrome OLED on I²C (addr 0x3C). Adafruit SSD1306 + GFX library. Display refreshed at 2 Hz; partial-update of changed lines only. Driven directly from 3.3 V GPIO; OLED supply via on-board charge pump.
rationale: SSD1306 is the most widely supported embedded display: ≥2000:1 contrast is readable in sunlight when enclosed; no backlight power; 1024-byte framebuffer fits in Arduino Nano SRAM. 2 Hz refresh is adequate for slowly-changing sensor readings and avoids OLED burn-in.

alternatives.reason_rejected: Requires 6+ GPIO lines or I²C expander; backlight draws 40 mA; poor sunlight contrast.
Only 32 rows — insufficient for multi-line layouts (speed + distance + trip + battery on one screen).

verification_notes: FACT: 'OLED SSD1306 Monochrome Display I²C Interface' confirms 128×64 resolution, I²C address 0x3C/0x3D, and Adafruit library compatibility.
supporting_fact_titles: OLED SSD1306 Monochrome Display I²C Interface
